title: Establishment and Morphological Characterization of Patient-Derived Organoids from Breast Cancer
author: Mazzucchelli, Piccotti, et al.
materials: Human breast primary tumor tissue from surgical specimen or from biopsy (fresh; the quantity usually varies).
Nalgene Cryo 1 °C freezing container filled with isopropanol (Nalgene #5100–0001).

Digest the minced tissue in digestion buffer (10 mL/g of tissue; DMEM-F/12 supplemented with 10 mM HEPES, 2% BSA, 0.5 μg/ml hydrocortisone, 50 μg/mL gentamycin) supplemented with Collagenase III (Wortington #LS004182; 0.2 mg/mL) and with Hyaluronidase (Sigma #H3884; 1000 U/mL) for 16 h at 37 °C under shaking (200 rpm).

Digest the minced tissue in 2 mL of digestion buffer (DMEM-F/12 supplemented with 10 mM HEPES, 2% BSA, 0.5 μg/ml hydrocortisone, 50 μg/mL gentamycin) supplemented with Collagenase III (Wortington #LS004182; 0.2 mg/mL) and with Hyaluronidase (Sigma #H3884; 1000 U/mL) for 3–4 h at 37 °C under shaking (200 rpm).
In the meantime, prepare Complete CCM.
Discard the supernatant leaving 1 mL of WM.
In the meantime, prepare Complete Culture Medium (CCM).
Invert the tube a few times.
Invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.

In the reference period, fresh tumor specimens from surgical resections have been collected from 24 patients affected by breast cancer at any stage and candidated to immediate breast-conserving surgery or total mastectomy. A written and oral informed consent to participate in the present protocol was obtained from each patient. Tumor specimens obtained from surgical resection could be stored at 4 °C in working medium (WM) until processing. Processing into organoids should be performed in sterile conditions within 48 h from the day of collection. All tissue processing was performed in a biosafety cabinet (hood) using Biosafety Level 2 (BSL2) techniques. The amount of tissue used for this procedure ranged from 0.17 g to 13.1 g.Transfer the solid tumor tissue in a 6 or 10 cm petri dish. Assure to keep the tissue moist with PBS.Remove fat tissue and mince tumor sample with scalpel by criss cross motion.Transfer the minced tissue in a 50 mL tube using a cell lifter and measure the net weight of the obtained tissue.Digest the minced tissue in digestion buffer (10 mL/g of tissue; DMEM-F/12 supplemented with 10 mM HEPES, 2% BSA, 0.5 μg/ml hydrocortisone, 50 μg/mL gentamycin) supplemented with Collagenase III (Wortington #LS004182; 0.2 mg/mL) and with Hyaluronidase (Sigma #H3884; 1000 U/mL) for 16 h at 37 °C under shaking (200 rpm).To remove undigested fragments and debris, filter on sterile 100 μm cell strainer and collect the flow-through sample (Fig. 12a). This medium mainly contains PDO, cancer single cells, red blood cells and fat.Place a sterile 20 μm cell strainer on a new 50 mL sterile tube and label the tube as single cells fraction.Discard 100 μm cell strainer and load the medium collected at passage 5 on the 20 μm cell strainer prepared in the previous step to separate single cells from organoids (Fig. 12b). After this passage organoids should be entrapped in the 20 μm cell strainer, while single cells should be collected in the tube.Invert the 20 μm cell strainer on a new 50 mL tube and wash twice with 5 mL of WM to collect tumor organoids entrapped inside the 20 μm grid (Fig. 12c).Discard the 20 μm cell strainer and collect organoids by centrifuging 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.Remove the supernatant avoiding to disrupt organoid pellet.If the pellet contains red blood cells (observed as a red layer on top of the pellet), add 1 mL of TAC buffer and incubate 10 min in a 37 °C water bath. Then, neutralize with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge (5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C) to collect organoids.If the pellet contains a lot of fat, perform one or two additional washing step with 10 mL of WM.Remove carefully the supernatant and resuspend organoid pellet in Matrigel 90% in WM (Corning, Matrigel Basement Membrane Matrix Growth Factor Reduced, Phenol Red Free, #356231) for seeding. Table 1 reports the volume of Matrigel necessary to seed organoids in plates of different formats. If the pellet is invisible, we advise to seed the sample in a well of 24-wells plate.Seed in a pre-warmed multiwell plate, then invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking. After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.In the meantime, prepare Complete Culture Medium (CCM) [6]. At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, invert the plate, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing (refer to Table 2 for volume of CCM required/plate size).Fig. 12Images of the filtration passages to obtain PDO from a surgical specimen. a Filtration throught 100 μm cell strainer. b Entrapment of organoids on top of a 20 μm cell strainer and c recovery of organoids from the top of a 20 μm cell strainerTable 1The volume of 90% Matrigel necessary to resuspend PDO before seeding in different plate format, established as μL /wellPlate formatμL of 90% Matrigel/well6-wells150 μL12-wells70 μL24-wells35 μLTable 2Summarizes the the volume of CCM required for each type of wellPlate formatmL of CCM/well6-wells1.5–2 mL12-wells1 mL24-wells0.5 mL

Fresh tumor biopsy of about 1.5 mm × 20 mm × 1.5 mm (2 samples obtained from a 12 gauge core needle biopsy) should be transferred into sterile WM and within 1 h from collection processed within 1 h from collection in sterile conditions in a biosafety cabinet (hood) using Biosafety Level 2 (BSL2) techniques.Transfer the solid tumor tissue in a 6 cm petri dish and assure to keep the tissue moist with PBS.Mince the tissue with scalpel by performing a criss cross motion.Transfer the minced tissue in a pre-weighted 15 mL tube using a cell lifter.Digest the minced tissue in 2 mL of digestion buffer (DMEM-F/12 supplemented with 10 mM HEPES, 2% BSA, 0.5 μg/ml hydrocortisone, 50 μg/mL gentamycin) supplemented with Collagenase III (Wortington #LS004182; 0.2 mg/mL) and with Hyaluronidase (Sigma #H3884; 1000 U/mL) for 3–4 h at 37 °C under shaking (200 rpm).To remove undigested fragments and debris, filter on 100 μm cell strainer and collect the floe-through sample.Wash the strainer with 5 mL WM.Repeat step 6.Collect organoids by 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C centrifugation.Remove the supernatant avoiding to disrupt organoid pellet.If the pellet contains red blood cells (observed as a red layer on top of the pellet), resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of TAC buffer and incubate 10 min in a 37 °C water bath. Then, neutralize with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge (5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C) to collect organoids.If the pellet contains a lot of fat, perform an additional washing step with 10 mL of WM.Remove carefully the supernatant and resuspend organoid pellet in Matrigel 90% in WM (Corning, Matrigel Basement Membrane Matrix Growth Factor Reduced, Phenol Red Free, #356231) for seeding. In Table 1 is reported the volume of Matrigel necessary to seed organoids in plates of different formats. If the pellet is invisible, we advise to seed the sample in a well of 24-wells plate.Seed in a pre-warmed multiwell plate, then invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking. After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.In the meantime, prepare CCM. At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer the plate back to the incubator for culturing (refer to Table 2 for CCM volume required/plate size).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Transfer the solid tumor tissue in a 6 or 10 cm petri dish. Assure to keep the tissue moist with PBS.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove fat tissue and mince tumor sample with scalpel by criss cross motion.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Transfer the minced tissue in a 50 mL tube using a cell lifter and measure the net weight of the obtained tissue.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: To remove undigested fragments and debris, filter on sterile 100 μm cell strainer and collect the flow-through sample (Fig. 12a). This medium mainly contains PDO, cancer single cells, red blood cells and fat.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Place a sterile 20 μm cell strainer on a new 50 mL sterile tube and label the tube as single cells fraction.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Discard 100 μm cell strainer and load the medium collected at passage 5 on the 20 μm cell strainer prepared in the previous step to separate single cells from organoids (Fig. 12b). After this passage organoids should be entrapped in the 20 μm cell strainer, while single cells should be collected in the tube.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Invert the 20 μm cell strainer on a new 50 mL tube and wash twice with 5 mL of WM to collect tumor organoids entrapped inside the 20 μm grid (Fig. 12c).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Discard the 20 μm cell strainer and collect organoids by centrifuging 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant avoiding to disrupt organoid pellet.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: If the pellet contains red blood cells (observed as a red layer on top of the pellet), add 1 mL of TAC buffer and incubate 10 min in a 37 °C water bath. Then, neutralize with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge (5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C) to collect organoids.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: If the pellet contains a lot of fat, perform one or two additional washing step with 10 mL of WM.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove carefully the supernatant and resuspend organoid pellet in Matrigel 90% in WM (Corning, Matrigel Basement Membrane Matrix Growth Factor Reduced, Phenol Red Free, #356231) for seeding. Table 1 reports the volume of Matrigel necessary to seed organoids in plates of different formats. If the pellet is invisible, we advise to seed the sample in a well of 24-wells plate.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Seed in a pre-warmed multiwell plate, then invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking. After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: In the meantime, prepare Complete Culture Medium (CCM) [6]. At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, invert the plate, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing (refer to Table 2 for volume of CCM required/plate size).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Transfer the solid tumor tissue in a 6 cm petri dish and assure to keep the tissue moist with PBS.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Mince the tissue with scalpel by performing a criss cross motion.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Transfer the minced tissue in a pre-weighted 15 mL tube using a cell lifter.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: To remove undigested fragments and debris, filter on 100 μm cell strainer and collect the floe-through sample.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash the strainer with 5 mL WM.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Repeat step 6.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Collect organoids by 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C centrifugation.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant avoiding to disrupt organoid pellet.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: If the pellet contains red blood cells (observed as a red layer on top of the pellet), resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of TAC buffer and incubate 10 min in a 37 °C water bath. Then, neutralize with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge (5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C) to collect organoids.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: If the pellet contains a lot of fat, perform an additional washing step with 10 mL of WM.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove carefully the supernatant and resuspend organoid pellet in Matrigel 90% in WM (Corning, Matrigel Basement Membrane Matrix Growth Factor Reduced, Phenol Red Free, #356231) for seeding. In Table 1 is reported the volume of Matrigel necessary to seed organoids in plates of different formats. If the pellet is invisible, we advise to seed the sample in a well of 24-wells plate.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Seed in a pre-warmed multiwell plate, then invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking. After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: In the meantime, prepare CCM. At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer the plate back to the incubator for culturing (refer to Table 2 for CCM volume required/plate size).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Using CCM remained in the plate, dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash twice the plate using fresh WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Resuspend in the appropriate amount of 90% Matrigel to expand the culture 1:2–1:3.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Seed organoids-matrigel drops and invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing (Fig. 13).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Using CCM remained in the plate, dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash twice the plate using fresh WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of TrypLE to obtain a WM:TrypLE ratio of 1:1

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Using a flamed glass Pasteur, pipette up-and down for 20 times organoid suspension to combine enzymatic shearing of TrypLE with mechanical shearing.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Check shearing process under the light microscope. If there aren’t signs of organoid rupture continue mechanic shearing pipetting up and down 10–15 times more.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Neutralize TrypLE with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge to collect sheared organoids.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend organoids in the appropriate amount of 90% Matrigel to expand the culture 1:2–1:3.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Seed organoids-matrigel drops and invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing (Fig. 14).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove CCM from the selected plate.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: To easily visualize and manipulate PDO drop label it by 30 min incubation with 0.05% crystal violet.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: At the end of incubation wash PDO thrice with PBS to remove the excess of crystal violet (Fig. 16).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: At the same time, dissolve agarose in milliQ water boiling the mixture to obtain a suspension of 2% agarose.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove a labelled drop of PDO with a cell lifter from the multi well plate and place it in a 15 × 15 × 5 mm mold pre-filled with unsolidified agarose 2%.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Place inside the agarose matrix about 3–4 Matrigel drops of PDO.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wait about 15 min to obtain complete solidification of 2% agarose matrix (Fig. 16).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove the agarose piece from the mold and place it in a histological cassette in an oriented way (Fig. 16).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Put the histological cassette in 10% buffered formalin for fixation (Fig. 16).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Fixation and paraffin embedding have been performed using histo star embedding workstation (ASP300 Leica). After 2 h of incubation in buffered Formalin 10%, the sample was dehydrated with 3 cycles of 1 h and one of 2 h in 95% ethanol, followed by 2 cycles of 1 h and one of 2 h in absolute ethanol. Then, the sample was subjected to 2 cycles of incubation in Bioclear (Bio-Optica) for 1 h each, followed by one cycle lasting 2 h. After that, the sample was included in paraffin wax. Inclusion has been performed by two cycle of 1 h followed by one of 2 h.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Cut paraffin-embedded PDO in histological slides of 3,5 μm and labelled with VENTANA, Bench Mark ULTRA following automatized standard protocols established for E-caderin, c-ErbB2, estrogen receptor, progesterone receptor and Ki67 immunohistochemistry (ICH). Clones of antibodies used for immunohistochemistry are reported in Table 3.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: At the end of ICH procedure, counter stain histological slides with eosin, dehydrate with ethanol (2 cycles in 95% ethanol and 2 cycles in 100% ethanol) and Bioclear and mount with a coverslip.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove a drop of PDO embedded in 90% Matrigel with a cell lifter from a multi well plate and place it in a 1.5 mL tube.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 2.5% glutaraldehyde in cacodylate buffer overnight.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash thrice with cacodylate buffer.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Fix with osmium tetroxide 1.5% in cacodylate buffer for 2 h.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash again with cacodylate buffer.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Dehydrate with an ascending ladder of ethanol from 50 to 100%.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Cut slides of 70–80 nm and label them with uranil acetate and lead citrate.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Acquire images with the Transmission Electron Microscope Tecnai Spirit BT FEI.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Using CCM remained in the plate, dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash twice the plate using fresh WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of TrypLE to obtain a WM:TrypLE ratio of 1:1.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Using a flamed glass Pasteur, pipette up-and down for 20 times organoid suspension to combine enzymatic shearing of TrypLE with mechanic shearing.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Check shearing process under the light microscope. If there aren’t signs of organoid rupture continue mechanic shearing pipetting up and down 10–15 times more.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Neutralize TrypLE with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge to collect sheared organoids.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend organoids in the appropriate amount of 90% Matrigel to re-seed them in a 6 multiwell plate.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Seed organoids-matrigel drops and invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: After Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer the plate into the incubator for culturing.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: After a couple of day collect organoids using CCM in the plate to dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new pre-chilled 15 mL tube. Use two 15 mL tubes to collect organoids from a full 6 multiwell plate.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Leave the tube on ice and wash twice the plate using Ice-cooled WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Add 11 mL of ice-cooled WM and incubate 10 min in ice. This incubation should help in dissolve Matrigel.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Leaving tubes in ice, pipet up and down and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and repeat the washing 18–20 if the suspension of Matrigel and organoids is more than 0.2 mL.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Leave the tubes in ice and add 3 mL of freezing medium in each 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Prepare 6 aliquots of PDO suspension from each 15 mL tube by pipetting 0.5 mL of PDO suspension into each cryovial.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Immediately transfer cryovials in a Nalgene cryostep for 24 h at − 80 °C, then store the aliquots in liquid nitrogen for long term conservation.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Thaw the vial rapidly by soaking in a 37 °C water bath under agitation until there is a piece of frozen material left.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove the vial from the water bath and clean it with ethanol before the transfer in a biosafety hood.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Transfer the thawed organoids to a sterile 15 ml falcon tube and add 1 ml of warm WM drop by drop while shaking the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Mix carefully by pipetting up and down a few times to dilute the freezing medium and slowly add 9 mL of warm WM into the 15 mL tube containing the organoids.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant without disrupting the pellet and resuspend the pellet in the same volume of 90% matrigel that was frozen in the vial (about 70 μL).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Seed crowded in a pre-warmed 24-multiwell plate to support organoids recovery from thawing procedure.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: After Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Follow organoids growth for some day to verify that the freezing/thawing process has been placed correctly.

steps.title: Protocol #1: Tissue digestion from breast cancer surgical specimen
steps.instruction: Transfer the solid tumor tissue in a 6 or 10 cm petri dish. Assure to keep the tissue moist with PBS.

steps.title: Protocol #1: Tissue digestion from breast cancer surgical specimen
steps.instruction: Remove fat tissue and mince tumor sample with scalpel by criss cross motion.

After this passage organoids should be entrapped in the 20 μm cell strainer, while single cells should be collected in the tube.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Once seeded in matrigel droplets, generally, Breast Cancer organoids should be passed every 2 weeks to prevent that they become too much (a) or too big (b). Two different procedures should be performed in each case. The procedure (a) should be followed when density in Matrigel drop is high but organoids are small (Fig. 13); the protocol (b) should be performed when organoids are big while not necessary too crowded (Fig. 14). An example of organoids growth rate is shown in Fig. 15.Fig. 13Images of PDO culture before and after expansionFig. 14Images of PDO culture before and after the shearing processFig. 15Schematic growth rate profile of three representative PDO cultures

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction:
Organoid expansion
Using CCM remained in the plate, dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new 15 mL tube.Wash twice the plate using fresh WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.Discard the supernatant.Resuspend in the appropriate amount of 90% Matrigel to expand the culture 1:2–1:3.Seed organoids-matrigel drops and invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.In the meantime, prepare Complete CCM.At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing (Fig. 13).
b)Organoid shearing

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: PDO from breast cancer are generally compact organoids, therefore we suggest to follow the procedure described below. Instead, in case of grape-like or non compact organoids, avoid the use of TrypLE and divide PDO by mechanical shearing only.Using CCM remained in the plate, dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new 15 mL tube.Wash twice the plate using fresh WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.Discard the supernatant leaving 1 mL of WM.Add 1 mL of TrypLE to obtain a WM:TrypLE ratio of 1:1Using a flamed glass Pasteur, pipette up-and down for 20 times organoid suspension to combine enzymatic shearing of TrypLE with mechanical shearing.Check shearing process under the light microscope. If there aren’t signs of organoid rupture continue mechanic shearing pipetting up and down 10–15 times more.Neutralize TrypLE with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge to collect sheared organoids.Discard the supernatant and resuspend organoids in the appropriate amount of 90% Matrigel to expand the culture 1:2–1:3.Seed organoids-matrigel drops and invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.In the meantime, prepare CCM.At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing (Fig. 14).

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Using CCM remained in the plate, dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Wash twice the plate using fresh WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Resuspend in the appropriate amount of 90% Matrigel to expand the culture 1:2–1:3.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Seed organoids-matrigel drops and invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing (Fig. 13).

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Using CCM remained in the plate, dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Wash twice the plate using fresh WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of TrypLE to obtain a WM:TrypLE ratio of 1:1

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Using a flamed glass Pasteur, pipette up-and down for 20 times organoid suspension to combine enzymatic shearing of TrypLE with mechanical shearing.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Check shearing process under the light microscope. If there aren’t signs of organoid rupture continue mechanic shearing pipetting up and down 10–15 times more.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Neutralize TrypLE with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge to collect sheared organoids.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend organoids in the appropriate amount of 90% Matrigel to expand the culture 1:2–1:3.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: Seed organoids-matrigel drops and invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Protocol #3: PDO culturing
steps.instruction: At the end of the process of Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing (Fig. 14).

steps.title: formalin fixation after the placement of...
steps.instruction:
formalin fixation after the placement of the agarose cube containing PDOs in the histological box
Table 3Summarizes the antibodies used for ICH with VENTANATargetCommercial namecloneProduct codec-erbB2PATHWAY anti-HER2/neu4B5 Rabbit Mab05278368001E-caderinE-caderinEP700Y Rabbit Mab05973872001Ki67CONFIRM anti-Ki6730–9 Rabbit Mab05278384001Estrogen ReceptorCONFIRM anti-ERSP1 Rabbit Mab05278406001Progesteron ReceptorCONFIRM anti-ER1E2 Rabbit Mab05277990001

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: Remove CCM from the selected plate.

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: To easily visualize and manipulate PDO drop label it by 30 min incubation with 0.05% crystal violet.

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: At the end of incubation wash PDO thrice with PBS to remove the excess of crystal violet (Fig. 16).

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: At the same time, dissolve agarose in milliQ water boiling the mixture to obtain a suspension of 2% agarose.

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: Remove a labelled drop of PDO with a cell lifter from the multi well plate and place it in a 15 × 15 × 5 mm mold pre-filled with unsolidified agarose 2%.

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: Place inside the agarose matrix about 3–4 Matrigel drops of PDO.

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: Wait about 15 min to obtain complete solidification of 2% agarose matrix (Fig. 16).

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: Remove the agarose piece from the mold and place it in a histological cassette in an oriented way (Fig. 16).

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: Put the histological cassette in 10% buffered formalin for fixation (Fig. 16).

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: Fixation and paraffin embedding have been performed using histo star embedding workstation (ASP300 Leica). After 2 h of incubation in buffered Formalin 10%, the sample was dehydrated with 3 cycles of 1 h and one of 2 h in 95% ethanol, followed by 2 cycles of 1 h and one of 2 h in absolute ethanol. Then, the sample was subjected to 2 cycles of incubation in Bioclear (Bio-Optica) for 1 h each, followed by one cycle lasting 2 h. After that, the sample was included in paraffin wax. Inclusion has been performed by two cycle of 1 h followed by one of 2 h.

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: Cut paraffin-embedded PDO in histological slides of 3,5 μm and labelled with VENTANA, Bench Mark ULTRA following automatized standard protocols established for E-caderin, c-ErbB2, estrogen receptor, progesterone receptor and Ki67 immunohistochemistry (ICH). Clones of antibodies used for immunohistochemistry are reported in Table 3.

steps.title: Protocol #4: PDO paraffin embedding for immunohistochemistry and histology
steps.instruction: At the end of ICH procedure, counter stain histological slides with eosin, dehydrate with ethanol (2 cycles in 95% ethanol and 2 cycles in 100% ethanol) and Bioclear and mount with a coverslip.

steps.title: Protocol #5: PDO processing for TEM imaging
steps.instruction: Remove a drop of PDO embedded in 90% Matrigel with a cell lifter from a multi well plate and place it in a 1.5 mL tube.Add 1 mL of 2.5% glutaraldehyde in cacodylate buffer overnight.Wash thrice with cacodylate buffer.Fix with osmium tetroxide 1.5% in cacodylate buffer for 2 h.Wash again with cacodylate buffer.Dehydrate with an ascending ladder of ethanol from 50 to 100%.Include in epon.Cut slides of 70–80 nm and label them with uranil acetate and lead citrate.Acquire images with the Transmission Electron Microscope Tecnai Spirit BT FEI.

steps.title: Protocol #5: PDO processing for TEM imaging
steps.instruction: Remove a drop of PDO embedded in 90% Matrigel with a cell lifter from a multi well plate and place it in a 1.5 mL tube.

steps.title: Protocol #5: PDO processing for TEM imaging
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 2.5% glutaraldehyde in cacodylate buffer overnight.

steps.title: Protocol #5: PDO processing for TEM imaging
steps.instruction: Wash thrice with cacodylate buffer.

steps.title: Protocol #5: PDO processing for TEM imaging
steps.instruction: Fix with osmium tetroxide 1.5% in cacodylate buffer for 2 h.

steps.title: Protocol #5: PDO processing for TEM imaging
steps.instruction: Wash again with cacodylate buffer.

steps.title: Protocol #5: PDO processing for TEM imaging
steps.instruction: Dehydrate with an ascending ladder of ethanol from 50 to 100%.

steps.title: Protocol #5: PDO processing for TEM imaging
steps.instruction: Cut slides of 70–80 nm and label them with uranil acetate and lead citrate.

steps.title: Protocol #5: PDO processing for TEM imaging
steps.instruction: Acquire images with the Transmission Electron Microscope Tecnai Spirit BT FEI.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Once obtained a 6 well plate full of organoids ranging from 40 to 100 μm, we could proceed to freezing. 2–3 days before freezing, one should share them in order to obtain a pool of dense and homogeneously small organoids.Using CCM remained in the plate, dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new 15 mL tube.Wash twice the plate using fresh WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.Discard the supernatant leaving 1 mL of WM.Add 1 mL of TrypLE to obtain a WM:TrypLE ratio of 1:1.Using a flamed glass Pasteur, pipette up-and down for 20 times organoid suspension to combine enzymatic shearing of TrypLE with mechanic shearing.Check shearing process under the light microscope. If there aren’t signs of organoid rupture continue mechanic shearing pipetting up and down 10–15 times more.Neutralize TrypLE with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge to collect sheared organoids.Discard the supernatant and resuspend organoids in the appropriate amount of 90% Matrigel to re-seed them in a 6 multiwell plate.Seed organoids-matrigel drops and invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.In the meantime, prepare Complete Culture Medium (CCM).After Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer the plate into the incubator for culturing.After a couple of day collect organoids using CCM in the plate to dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new pre-chilled 15 mL tube. Use two 15 mL tubes to collect organoids from a full 6 multiwell plate.Leave the tube on ice and wash twice the plate using Ice-cooled WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.Discard the supernatant leaving 1 mL of WM.Add 11 mL of ice-cooled WM and incubate 10 min in ice. This incubation should help in dissolve Matrigel.Leaving tubes in ice, pipet up and down and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.Discard the supernatant and repeat the washing 18–20 if the suspension of Matrigel and organoids is more than 0.2 mL.Leave the tubes in ice and add 3 mL of freezing medium in each 15 mL tube.Prepare 6 aliquots of PDO suspension from each 15 mL tube by pipetting 0.5 mL of PDO suspension into each cryovial.Immediately transfer cryovials in a Nalgene cryostep for 24 h at − 80 °C, then store the aliquots in liquid nitrogen for long term conservation.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Using CCM remained in the plate, dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Wash twice the plate using fresh WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of TrypLE to obtain a WM:TrypLE ratio of 1:1.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Using a flamed glass Pasteur, pipette up-and down for 20 times organoid suspension to combine enzymatic shearing of TrypLE with mechanic shearing.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Check shearing process under the light microscope. If there aren’t signs of organoid rupture continue mechanic shearing pipetting up and down 10–15 times more.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Neutralize TrypLE with 10 mL of WM and centrifuge to collect sheared organoids.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend organoids in the appropriate amount of 90% Matrigel to re-seed them in a 6 multiwell plate.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Seed organoids-matrigel drops and invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: After Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer the plate into the incubator for culturing.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: After a couple of day collect organoids using CCM in the plate to dissolve the Matrigel drop and transfer the medium containing Matrigel and organoids in a new pre-chilled 15 mL tube. Use two 15 mL tubes to collect organoids from a full 6 multiwell plate.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Leave the tube on ice and wash twice the plate using Ice-cooled WM (at least 2 mL to wash a well of 6 multiwell plate)

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Collect washes and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Add 11 mL of ice-cooled WM and incubate 10 min in ice. This incubation should help in dissolve Matrigel.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Leaving tubes in ice, pipet up and down and centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and repeat the washing 18–20 if the suspension of Matrigel and organoids is more than 0.2 mL.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Leave the tubes in ice and add 3 mL of freezing medium in each 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Prepare 6 aliquots of PDO suspension from each 15 mL tube by pipetting 0.5 mL of PDO suspension into each cryovial.

steps.title: Protocol #6: PDO freezing
steps.instruction: Immediately transfer cryovials in a Nalgene cryostep for 24 h at − 80 °C, then store the aliquots in liquid nitrogen for long term conservation.

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: The day before the thawing of an organoid vial it is necessary to pre-warm a 24-well tissue culture plate in the incubator.Pre-warm WM at 37 °C before use.Thaw the vial rapidly by soaking in a 37 °C water bath under agitation until there is a piece of frozen material left.Remove the vial from the water bath and clean it with ethanol before the transfer in a biosafety hood.Transfer the thawed organoids to a sterile 15 ml falcon tube and add 1 ml of warm WM drop by drop while shaking the bottom of the tube.Mix carefully by pipetting up and down a few times to dilute the freezing medium and slowly add 9 mL of warm WM into the 15 mL tube containing the organoids.Invert the tube a few times.Centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °CDiscard the supernatant without disrupting the pellet and resuspend the pellet in the same volume of 90% matrigel that was frozen in the vial (about 70 μL).Seed crowded in a pre-warmed 24-multiwell plate to support organoids recovery from thawing procedure.Invert the plate to avoid organoid sinking.After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.In the meantime, prepare CCM.After Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing.Follow organoids growth for some day to verify that the freezing/thawing process has been placed correctly.

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: Thaw the vial rapidly by soaking in a 37 °C water bath under agitation until there is a piece of frozen material left.

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: Remove the vial from the water bath and clean it with ethanol before the transfer in a biosafety hood.

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: Transfer the thawed organoids to a sterile 15 ml falcon tube and add 1 ml of warm WM drop by drop while shaking the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: Mix carefully by pipetting up and down a few times to dilute the freezing medium and slowly add 9 mL of warm WM into the 15 mL tube containing the organoids.

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 min at 500×g, T = 8 °C

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant without disrupting the pellet and resuspend the pellet in the same volume of 90% matrigel that was frozen in the vial (about 70 μL).

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: Seed crowded in a pre-warmed 24-multiwell plate to support organoids recovery from thawing procedure.

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: After 10 min, transfer the seeded plate into the incubator for 20 min, to allow Matrigel solidification.

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: After Matrigel solidification, gently add CCM to organoid-matrigel drops and transfer into incubator for culturing.

steps.title: Protocol #7: PDO thawing
steps.instruction: Follow organoids growth for some day to verify that the freezing/thawing process has been placed correctly.
